ESCAPE_CHARACTER.description: A single backslash preceding a custom macro syntax character.
ESCAPE_CHARACTER.behavior: Prevents the lexer from interpreting the subsequent character as a syntax operator. Standard escape sequences (e.g., \n, \t) not preceding a syntax marker are treated as raw literal text unless inside a Regex block or an Anonymous Escape Block.
ESCAPE_CHARACTER.outcome: Outputs `<bracket> and a path C:\new` without initiating an AST node capture or destroying the file path.

INDEXED_BLOCK_ROUTER.context: Top-level raw string inputs (prior to AST evaluation).
INDEXED_BLOCK_ROUTER.description: A line starting and ending with one or more hash `#` characters, optionally surrounding a title, with optional whitespace padding.
INDEXED_BLOCK_ROUTER.behavior: Splits multi-line strings into routed blocks. Accessed via positive (Preamble + Block) or negative (Block Only) integer routing.
INDEXED_BLOCK_ROUTER.outcome: Isolates 'Text block here.' for injection into the engine.

LORA_WRAPPER.description: Square brackets enclosing a literal group name, a colon, and the native ComfyUI `<lora:...>` string.
LORA_WRAPPER.behavior: Protects native ComfyUI syntax from macro evaluation, routes the payload to the Trace State Object under `group_name`, and strips the bracket wrapper for final output.

2_STATE_MUTATIONS_DEFINITIONS.ORTHOGONAL_MATRIX_RULES: Definitions follow [CLASS][KEY_PATTERN][POSITION][STRENGTH][VALUE_PATTERN] syntax. CLASS, POSITION and STRENGTH determine evaluation behavior; KEY_PATTERN and VALUE_PATTERN can each be either literal text or a regex (/pattern/). Definitions must appear at the start of a parsed line or segment, and for the time being, the VALUE_PATTERN strictly terminates at the End-of-Line (\n).

SYNTAX_COMPOSITION_TABLE.description: Complete list of valid definition syntax patterns

BOUNDED_WEAK.behavior: Used only when no strong definition exists for the key.

PRE_PATTERN_STRONG.behavior: Applied left-to-right against raw input before AST lexing.

PRE_PATTERN_WEAK.behavior: Applied left-to-right against raw input, lower priority than strong.

POST_PATTERN_STRONG.behavior: Applied left-to-right against final output after AST resolution.

POST_PATTERN_WEAK.behavior: Applied left-to-right against final output, lower priority than strong.

CLASS_PRE_PATTERN.description: A colon followed immediately by a left angle bracket. Marks unbounded pre-pattern evaluation.
CLASS_PRE_PATTERN.behavior: Applied Left-to-Right against raw strings before AST lexing.

CLASS_BOUNDED.description: A single colon. Marks bounded evaluation (applies only to tokens within `< >`).
CLASS_BOUNDED.behavior: Evaluated exclusively within `< >` AST macro boundaries.

CLASS_POST_PATTERN.description: A colon followed immediately by a right angle bracket. Marks unbounded post-pattern evaluation.
CLASS_POST_PATTERN.behavior: Applied Left-to-Right against flattened strings after AST resolution.

POSITION_LEFT.description: A left angle bracket. Marks that result will be appended to left of the base output.
POSITION_LEFT.behavior: For bounded definitions, appends result to the left of the base output and earlier LEFT definitions. For unbounded, shortand to add \g<0> to the right of the given pattern.

POSITION_BASE.description: An empty string. Marks a standard BASE definition that replaces the key with the value in the standard way.

POSITION_RIGHT.description: A right angle bracket. Marks that result will be appended to right of the base output.
POSITION_RIGHT.behavior: For bounded definitions, appends result to the right of the base output and earlier RIGHT definitions. For unbounded, shortand to add \g<0> to the left of the given pattern.

STRENGTH_STRONG.description: A single colon between KEY and VALUE markers.
STRENGTH_STRONG.behavior: Pushed to the Head of the Context Stack. Evaluated first in the Left-to-Right search. If it is a BASE position, it instantly terminates the search.

STRENGTH_WEAK.description: Two colons between KEY and VALUE markers.
STRENGTH_WEAK.behavior: Pushed to the Tail of the Context Stack. Acts as a fallback. Evaluated after strong definitions in the Left-to-Right search. If it is a BASE position, it terminates the search.

PATTERN_TYPE_REGEX.description: A string enclosed entirely by forward slashes. Applied to both KEY and VALUE independently.
PATTERN_TYPE_REGEX.behavior: Parsed as a Regular Expression. Supports native Python regex syntax, backreferences, and named groups.

PATTERN_TYPE_LITERAL.description: Any string not enclosed by forward slashes. Applied to both KEY and VALUE independently.
PATTERN_TYPE_LITERAL.behavior: Exact string matching (for KEY) or literal replacement (for VALUE).

EXAMPLES.description: Bounded, Strong, Literal Key, Literal Value.
Bounded, Weak, Literal Key, Literal Value.
EXAMPLES.outcome: Fallback: if no strong `:man:...` definition exists, `<man>` outputs `gentleman`.

EXAMPLES.description: Pre-pattern, Strong, Literal Key, Literal Value.
EXAMPLES.outcome: Applied before AST lexing: replaces 'dark' with 'light' in raw input.

EXAMPLES.description: Pre-pattern, Weak, Literal Key, Literal Value.
EXAMPLES.outcome: Applied before AST lexing with lower priority than strong pre-patterns.

EXAMPLES.description: Pre-pattern, Strong, Regex Key, Regex Value.
EXAMPLES.outcome: Regex pattern matching; replaces matched 'dark' with the literal string 'light'.

EXAMPLES.description: Pre-pattern, Weak, Regex Key, Regex Value.
EXAMPLES.outcome: Regex matching with weak priority; useful as fallback before AST evaluation.

EXAMPLES.description: Bounded, Strong, Literal Key, Regex Value.
EXAMPLES.outcome: Overrides `<man>` to output `gentleman` followed by a newline.

EXAMPLES.description: Post-pattern, Strong, Regex Key, Literal Value.
EXAMPLES.outcome: Applied after AST resolution: collapses multiple spaces into single space.

EXAMPLES.description: Post-pattern, Weak, Regex Key, Literal Value.
EXAMPLES.outcome: Applied after AST resolution with lower priority; acts as cleanup fallback.

STANDARD_INVOCATION.description: A literal token key surrounded by angle brackets.
STANDARD_INVOCATION.behavior: Searches Context Stack Left-to-Right. Replaces token with resolved AST of matching definition.

PARAMETERIZED_INVOCATION.description: A key, followed by a pipe `|`, followed by argument definitions separated by pipes, enclosed in angle brackets.
PARAMETERIZED_INVOCATION.behavior: Pushes `arg1` as a STRONG definition to local scope, evaluates `<key>`, then pops `arg1`.

DEFAULT_PARAMETER_INVOCATION.description: Same as parameterized, but using a double colon `::` for the argument definition.
DEFAULT_PARAMETER_INVOCATION.behavior: Pushes `arg1` as a WEAK definition to local scope, evaluates `<key>`, then pops `arg1`.
DEFAULT_PARAMETER_INVOCATION.outcome: Evaluates `subject` with `color` as `yellow` ONLY IF no higher-priority definition exists.

ATOMIC_ASSIGN_AND_RETURN.description: A literal key and literal value separated by a single colon, enclosed in angle brackets.
ATOMIC_ASSIGN_AND_RETURN.behavior: Simultaneously pushes `key:value` as a STRONG definition (Head of stack) and resolves the AST node to `value`.
ATOMIC_ASSIGN_AND_RETURN.outcome: Outputs 'The gentleman arrived' and registers `man:gentleman` as a strong definition for subsequent sibling nodes.

ATOMIC_ASSIGN_DEFAULT_AND_RETURN.description: A literal key and literal value separated by a double colon, enclosed in angle brackets.
ATOMIC_ASSIGN_DEFAULT_AND_RETURN.behavior: Simultaneously pushes `key:value` as a WEAK definition (Head of stack) and resolves the AST node to `value`.
ATOMIC_ASSIGN_DEFAULT_AND_RETURN.outcome: Outputs 'The guy arrived' and registers `man:guy` as a weak definition for subsequent sibling nodes.

ANONYMOUS_ESCAPE_BLOCK.description: An invocation where the key is wrapped exactly in forward slashes, containing standard Python string escape sequences.
ANONYMOUS_ESCAPE_BLOCK.behavior: Bypasses the Context Stack lookup. Strips the outer slashes and decodes the inner payload using Python's native unicode_escape to safely resolve literal bytes (like newlines or tabs) at evaluation time.
ANONYMOUS_ESCAPE_BLOCK.outcome: Outputs 'Line one.' followed by an actual newline character, then 'Line two.'

SCOPE_SIBLINGS.description: A key, followed by a pipe `|`, followed by invocations separated by pipes, enclosed in angle brackets.
SCOPE_SIBLINGS.behavior: The `|` hoists standard `ENTER_SCOPE`/`EXIT_SCOPE` behavior from `MACRO` invocations. Macros share a continuous local scope block. `MacroA` is evaluated first, then `MacroB`, with any definitions from `MacroA` still active during `MacroB` evaluation, continuing for any further invocations, then finally `key` is resolved with the context intact.
SCOPE_SIBLINGS.outcome: Definitions hoisted by `<SetNumber>` remain active and accessible during `<SetObject>` evaluation, then definitions from both are active during `<photo>` evaluation, popping only when the outer `< >` terminates.

TRANSPARENT_BLOCK_DUMMY_ROOT.description: An opening angle bracket followed immediately by a pipe `|`, followed by macro invocations separated by pipes, closed by an angle bracket.
TRANSPARENT_BLOCK_DUMMY_ROOT.behavior: The empty string before the first `|` hoists standard `ENTER_SCOPE`/`EXIT_SCOPE` behavior on the outer `<>` block as well as on `MacroA` and `MacroB`. The definitions from `MacroA` and `MacroB` remain active and accessible during the evaluation of the invoking node.
TRANSPARENT_BLOCK_DUMMY_ROOT.outcome: Definitions hoisted by `<SetColors>` remain active and accessible during later evaluations, popping only when the current node terminates.

INLINE_LOCAL_DEFINITION.description: Same as the Transparent Block Dummy Root, but with a single argument definition literal instead of macro invocation.
INLINE_LOCAL_DEFINITION.behavior: Pushes `arg1` as a WEAK definition to local scope.
INLINE_LOCAL_DEFINITION.example: The red <|:otherColor:blue> ball and after some extra long descriptive text that makes it hard to keep track of the <otherColor> cube.
INLINE_LOCAL_DEFINITION.outcome: Evaluates as `The red ball and ... the blue cube.`, with both colors being input together but output far apart.

SCOPED_PATTERN.description: Same as the Parameterized Invocation, but with an unbounded replacement.
SCOPED_PATTERN.behavior: Adds the pattern-replacement definition to the internally scoped context then evaluates `<key>`, finally popping the definition.
SCOPED_PATTERN.outcome: Evaluates as `My friendly pet cat saw their dogs.`, with the pattern applying to the <myDog> invocation but not elsewhere.

STANDARD_RANDOM.description: Multiple string options separated by pipes `|`, enclosed in curly braces.
STANDARD_RANDOM.behavior: Selects one option using the deterministic path-hashed PRNG.

QUANTITY_MODIFIER.description: An integer followed by a double dollar sign `$$`, preceding the piped options inside curly braces.
QUANTITY_MODIFIER.behavior: Selects `qty` number of options without replacement. Default separator is ', '.

CUSTOM_SEPARATOR.description: An integer, `$$`, a literal string separator, `$$`, and the piped options.

DISCRETE_SEQUENCE_GENERATOR.description: Colon-separated integers preceding the `$$` in a multi-value group.
DISCRETE_SEQUENCE_GENERATOR.behavior: Generates a discrete list of valid indices/quantities to be fed into the PRNG prior to item selection.
DISCRETE_SEQUENCE_GENERATOR.outcome: Randomly selects 1, 2, or 3 items from the pool.

KEY_INDIRECTION.description: A string token, bounded by angle brackets, containing literal text and another token invocation in angle brackets.
KEY_INDIRECTION.behavior: Resolves the inner `my_<metaKey>` to a flat string literal first by evaluating it, and then resolves the outer `< >` as a standard invocation using that resolved key.
KEY_INDIRECTION.outcome: Outputs `I love my small pet rat and my big pet dog.` after evaluating the inner keys to `my_rat` and `my_dog`, then resolving those as standard invocations.

KEY_MODIFICATION.description: A key inside curly braces, followed by a pipe `|`, followed by a pre-pattern definition, all enclosed in angle brackets.
KEY_MODIFICATION.behavior: Adds the prepattern to the shared scope, then recognizes the curly braces in the first segment as containing resolvable text rather than a literal key, resolves it to its single option, applies the pre-pattern substitution to that string, then resolves the resulting string as a standard invocation.
KEY_MODIFICATION.outcome: Evaluates `<bat>` to get defined value, then outputs that. With the start-anchored pre-pattern `/^ca/` added to the shared scope, it replaces `ca` with `ba` in evaluating the key string, but is unlikely to false-positive match the value string for `bat`, then it goes out of scope

KEY_RANDOMIZATION.description: A key with literal text followed by a set of alternatives inside curly braces.
KEY_RANDOMIZATION.behavior: Picks an option by standard seeded-randomization rules, then resolves the resulting key as a standard invocation. The randomization occurs at the key level, so it does not affect the resolution of the value or the application of patterns within that value.
KEY_RANDOMIZATION.outcome: Randomly selects one of the alternatives and then resolves the reulting evaluated key as a standard invocation.

POSITIONAL_ARGUMENTS.description: Numeric digits enclosed in angle brackets.
POSITIONAL_ARGUMENTS.behavior: Accessing parameterized arguments by integer index rather than explicit key string.

REGEX_CAPTURE_INTEROPERABILITY.description: Native regex capture groups and backreferences used within macro boundaries.
REGEX_CAPTURE_INTEROPERABILITY.behavior: Seamless interoperability between macro argument scopes and native regex capture groups. For example, mapping named regex capture groups `(?P<name>...)` on a key pattern directly to local `<name>` invocations within the replacement value.

INDEX_MASKING.description: A leading integer (or range) prefixed with a dollar sign `$`, followed by standard quantity modifiers.
INDEX_MASKING.behavior: Restricting the PRNG pool to specific indices of the provided options before selection.
